gaps_cron_to_langgraph.description: No built-in checkpointer; retry/idempotency are BYO via status table
gaps_cron_to_langgraph.mitigation: Adopt documented self-healing pattern with execution IDs

gaps_cron_to_langgraph.description: 5000 ms Edge Function timeout via Supabase Cron UI
gaps_cron_to_langgraph.mitigation: Use pgmq queue for jobs > 5s; cron enqueues, worker drains
Status-table state machine; sufficient for deterministic ETL

gaps_cron_to_langgraph.description: Single Postgres cluster — no cross-cluster orchestration
gaps_cron_to_langgraph.mitigation: Not relevant for Zona Sul scope; revisit if multi-region

gaps_cron_to_langgraph.description: Hand-off pattern adds a hop when Wave B NLP node lives in LangGraph subgraph
gaps_cron_to_langgraph.mitigation: AppFolio Realm-X uses exactly this pattern in production — well-precedented

gaps_langgraph_to_cron.description: $70+/mês platform fees for 10k×7=70k node execs on Plus (after free 100k tier)
gaps_langgraph_to_cron.mitigation: Don't use LangGraph for deterministic ETL — restrict to LLM nodes

gaps_langgraph_to_cron.description: Adds Python runtime to TS-first stack (or use less mature langgraph-js)
gaps_langgraph_to_cron.mitigation: Reserve Python container for ML/NLP only; not for ETL

gaps_langgraph_to_cron.description: Checkpointer saves state BETWEEN nodes, not INSIDE long-running nodes
gaps_langgraph_to_cron.mitigation: For deterministic ETL, decompose to small nodes (~< 1 min each); for crash-survival of long ops, pair with Temporal

gaps_langgraph_to_cron.description: Non-determinism is supported by design — not the right tool for deterministic ETL
gaps_langgraph_to_cron.mitigation: Match tool to problem class: deterministic→cron-Supabase, agentic→LangGraph

gaps_langgraph_to_cron.description: New mental model + observability vendor for the team
gaps_langgraph_to_cron.mitigation: Adopt only when LLM steps justify it (Wave B)

summary.interpretation: Both tools are excellent at their proper job class. cron-Supabase fits deterministic ETL of Wave A; LangGraph fits agentic/LLM reasoning of Wave B. Mixing them HAPPENS, but only at the boundary.
